programme.title: Hudson & Rex

programme.title: Hudson & Rex

programme.title: Hudson & Rex

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Charlie's Aunt Miranda and her friends are caught investigating an investment fraud scheme, so it's up to Charlie and Rex to get to the source of the fraud.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: When Jesse's sister contacts him for help, Jesse, Sarah and Rex head to Northern Ontario and find themselves in the middle of a deadly situation.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Rex seeks help from Detective Sidney Scott to save his stranded and injured friends.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Charlie gets a break in a long-unsolved kidnapping case that has haunted him, but runs into trouble when the abductee refuses his help.

programme.title: Single Moms Club
programme.desc: When five single moms form a support group, they find inspiration and laughter in their new sisterhood.

programme.title: Madea's Family Reunion
programme.desc: A take-no-trash-from-anybody matriarch tries to throw a family reunion to hold her family together.

programme.title: I Can Do Bad All by Myself
programme.desc: When Madea catches kids looting her home, she decides to take matters into her own hands.

programme.title: Madea Goes to Jail
programme.desc: Mischievous grandma Madea lands in jail, where she meets a variety of mixed-up characters.

programme.title: Peeples
programme.desc: Wade Walker crashes the Peeples annual reunion to ask for their precious daughter Grace's hand in marriage.

programme.title: Arlington Road [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Widowed when his FBI agent wife is killed by an extremist group, college professor Michael Faraday (Jeff Bridges) becomes obsessed with the culture of these groups -- especially when his new all-American neighbors, Oliver (Tim Robbins) and Cheryl Lang (Joan Cusack), start acting suspiciously. With each twist, the mystery deepens and questions loom whether Faraday is consumed by fear and driven by paranoia, or whether a lethal conspiracy has been born on Arlington Road.

programme.title: Limitless
programme.desc: Facing unemployment and his girlfriend's rejection, writer Eddie Morra is sure that he has no future. That all changes when an old friend gives him a drug that produces enhanced mental acuity. Stoked on the untested chemical, Eddie rises to the top of the financial world and attracts the attention of a tycoon who intends to use him to make a fortune. But terrible side effects and a dwindling supply threaten to collapse Eddie's house of cards.

programme.title: The Code
programme.desc: A veteran thief recruits a younger crook to help him pull off one final job in order to repay his debt to the Russian mob.

programme.title: 10th & Wolf [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: While serving in the military, a Marine sergeant (James Marsden) believes his family's ties to organized crime are a thing of the past. However, an act of insubordination and a visit from the F.B.I. send him packing to his South Philadelphia hometown, and he discovers that his brother (Brad Renfro) and his cousin (Giovanni Ribisi) have become entangled with mobsters, leaving him with only one way to save them.

programme.title: Season Of The Witch
programme.desc: 14th-century knights transport a suspected witch to a monastery, where monks deduce that her powers.

programme.title: Limitless
programme.desc: A mysterious pill that enables the user to access 100% of his brain's abilities transforms a struggling writer into a financial wizard, but it also puts him in a new world with many dangers.

programme.title: The Kill Room
programme.desc: A hitman, his boss, an art dealer and a money-laundering scheme that accidentally turns the assassin

programme.title: The Infiltrator
programme.desc: A U.S. Customs official uncovers a money laundering scheme involving Colombian drug lord Pablo Escobar.

programme.title: Sahara
programme.desc: After the fall of Tobruk in June 1942, U.S. Army sergeant Joe Gunn leads his tank into the Sahara desert.

programme.title: The Principal
programme.desc: At the toughest high school in town, the biggest troublemaker is the principal. Edited for TV

programme.title: Crossroads
programme.desc: One was a living legend, the other was just starting out... for both, the turning point will come at the Crossroads. Edited for TV

programme.title: Aloha
programme.desc: In Aloha, a celebrated military contractor (Bradley Cooper) returns to the site of his greatest career triumphs – the US Space program in Honolulu, Hawaii – and reconnects with a long-ago love (Rachel McAdams) while unexpectedly falling for the hard-charging Air Force watchdog (Emma Stone) assigned to him. From Academy Award-winner Cameron Crowe, the writer-director behind such films as Jerry Maguire and Almost Famous, Aloha also stars Bill Murray, John Krasinski, Danny McBride, and Alec Baldwin.

programme.title: The Shallows
programme.desc: In the taut thriller, when Nancy (Blake Lively) is surfing on a secluded beach, she finds herself on the feeding ground of a great white shark. Though she is stranded only 200 yards from shore, survival proves to be the ultimate test of wills, requiring all of Nancy s ingenuity, resourcefulness, and fortitude.

programme.title: Born Free
programme.desc: The true story of Joy Adamson and her husband, George, who adopt a slain lioness' cubs.

programme.title: A Night To Remember
programme.desc: Mystery writer Jeff Troy (Brian Aherne) and his lovely wife Nancy (Loretta Young) move into a Greenwich Village basement apartment in order to gather material for Jeff's new book.

programme.title: The Whole Truth
programme.desc: Movie producer Granger is bored in his marriage to Reed. He has an affair with Canale, a temperamental actress, but decides he prefers Reed's company. After he dumps Canale, detective tells him that the star has been stabbed, and all clues point toward Granger as the killer.

programme.title: Bye Bye Birdie
programme.desc: A rock singer travels to a small Ohio town to make his "farewell" television performance and kiss his biggest fan before he is drafted.

programme.title: The Bodyguard (1973)
programme.desc: Karate master and anti-drug vigilante Kiba returns to his home in Japan, where he aims to wipe out the nation's drug industry.

programme.title: The Gambler II: The Adventure Continues (Part 1)
programme.desc: Brady Hawkes, Billy Montana, and Jeremiah Hawkes are on a train bound for a huge gambling event when the train is taken over by a gang of vicious killers in search of money. As ransom, the gang takes young Jeremiah hostage. Brady and Billy embark on a quest to rescue him and form a small gang of their own along the way.

programme.title: Metroland
programme.desc: After ten years absence Toni, Chris's best friend, suddenly reappears in London to bring chaos and doubt into Chris's calm, tranquil, slightly boring, predictable life. Chris starts to remember his carefree youth as a photographer in Paris when he lived with and enjoyed a torrid affair with Annick. It was also in Paris that he first met and fell in love with Marion. The temptations and pressure exerted on Chris by Toni to return to their former carefree life of sex, drugs and rock'n'roll soon starts to have an impact on Chris's marriage. He starts to question his values, his lifestyle choices and his relationship with Marion and even suspects her of starting an affair with Toni whom she dislikes! Eventually circumstances come to a head and Chris is forced to decide whether to follow Toni back to the hedonistic, irresponsible life of his youth or face the harsh realities of the present and stay with Marion.

programme.title: The Road To Wellville
programme.desc: Presiding over the renowned Battle Creek Sanitarium is the visionary cornflake inventor and health-craze huckster Dr. John Harvey Kellogg (Hopkins). Seeking to improve their health – and their marriage – are Eleanor (Fonda), Kellogg's spellbound devotee, and Will (Broderick), her reluctant but devoted husband. What doesn't kill them in this spa/hospital/grand hotel will only make them stronger as they make their way through an obstacle course of sexual temptations, mechanical contraptions and dietary breakthroughs. Filled with outrageous methodology and hilarious hijinks, this "sumptuously photographed turn-of-the-century comedy with a fine cast" (San Francisco Chronicle) will make you double over ... with laughter!

programme.title: Den of Thieves
programme.desc: A cop and an ex-military gang leader clash when the Outlaws plan a heist at L.A.'s Federal Reserve Bank.

programme.title: Erased
programme.desc: Ex-CIA operative Ben Logan has been long estranged from his young daughter.

programme.title: American Ultra
programme.desc: A small-town slacker discovers he's a sleeper agent targeted by CIA operatives.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: A 24 hour "kill or die" tournament is held in an English town. The winner will claim a $10,000,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Prey of Wrath
programme.desc: An elite special forces operative is sent by Russian Intelligence to hunt down former teammates who have hijacked nuclear warheads.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark 2
programme.desc: A new victim has been chosen to face off against a ravenous shark in a Texas lake.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark 3
programme.desc: Two female survivors join forces to bring down the deadly group responsible for unleashing a hungry shark in a West Texas lake.

programme.title: The Wide West
programme.desc: 1907. A mysterious gambler aids a couple of Japanese sumo wrestlers, the local Judge and a beautiful store owner against ruthless outlaws who have attacked a small Californian town.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark
programme.desc: A group of virtual hunters unleash a deadly shark in a Texas lake, causing a young couple to fight for their lives during a peaceful fishing trip in a secluded cove.

programme.title: The Rise of Hero
programme.desc: Huang Feihong wants to make a living in Guangzhou. With the help of Zhou Shisan and Lu Yibai, he is finally allowed to take an exam in Guangzhou, and he may be able to study abroad. But after he gets there, he discovers a conspiracy among city leaders and the conservatives in the court led by Eunuch Hai, who wants to end the Studying Abroad Campaign launched by the Westernization Movement.

programme.title: Shaolin
programme.desc: After a shocking betrayal by his sworn brother, a general (Andy Lau) takes refuge at a Shaolin temple and learns martial arts from a crazy cook (Jackie Chan).

programme.title: Alienoid
programme.desc: Gurus in the late Goryeo dynasty try to obtain a fabled, holy sword, and humans in 2022 hunt down an alien prisoner that is locked in a human's body. The two parties cross paths when a time-traveling portal opens up.

programme.title: The Brink
programme.desc: A reckless police inspector is on a mission to crack down on a criminal's gold smuggling scheme.

programme.title: Escaping The Madhouse: The Nellie Bly Story
programme.desc: On a mission to expose the deplorable conditions and mistreatment of patients.

programme.title: Fatal Honeymoon
programme.desc: True story: Newlywed dies on honeymoon, husband's guilt investigated.

programme.title: My Life is a Lifetime Movie
programme.desc: Following a difficult breakup in a string of failed relationships.

programme.title: An Officer and a Murderer
programme.desc: A popular Canadian Air Force Officer is shockingly arrested.

programme.title: Escaping The Madhouse: The Nellie Bly Story
programme.desc: On a mission to expose the deplorable conditions and mistreatment of patients.

programme.title: Fatal Honeymoon
programme.desc: True story: Newlywed dies on honeymoon, husband's guilt investigated.

programme.title: FBI: Negotiator
programme.desc: An FBI agent must negotiate with a woman holding a hospital hostage in order to get her hands on an experimental drug.

programme.title: Wild Faith
programme.desc: A Civil War vet makes peace with his losses and starts a new life with the world resisting change around him.

programme.title: WWJD? The Journey Continues
programme.desc: WWJD continues as the drifter arrives into a new town , inspiring a group of people to live as Jesus would.

programme.title: Heartfelt
programme.desc: Molly hurries to boyfriend Jackson's birthday party with some big news to deliver.

programme.title: Finding Love in Saint Lucia
programme.desc: An Ornithologist and a Journalist partner up to save an endangered bird species in the jungle of Saint Lucia.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Former Marine John "Falcon" Chapman is a dark anti-hero driven by guilt and suffering from PTSD, who will destroy himself unless given something else to destroy - a useful weapon-of-last-resort for the U.S. State Department.

programme.title: Traffickers
programme.desc: Tiffany Cooper, disgraced by the military, wanted by the cops and running out of time, stops at nothing to take down a Trafficking Operation and save these young girls. Nothing is what it seems when everyone seems to be against you. Trust No One.

programme.title: American Honey
programme.desc: Star (Sasha Lane), an adolescent girl from a troubled home, runs away with a traveling sales crew that drives across the American Midwest selling subscriptions door-to-door. Finding her feet in this gang of teenagers, one of whom is Jake (Shia LaBeouf), she soon gets into the group's lifestyle of hard-partying nights, law-bending days and young love.

programme.title: I'll Be There
programme.desc: More than a decade after distancing herself from her complicated family, Grace's carefully built life is upended when her brother AJ is diagnosed with cancer. As her estranged siblings reunite in Los Angeles, Grace is forced to confront old wounds, buried loyalties, and the role she once played in her fractured family-set against the surreal backdrop of Michael Jackson's death.

programme.title: Murder Without Motive
programme.desc: Edmund Perry has everything going for him after he graduates with a scholarship to attend Stanford. With his dedication paying off, an encounter with a police officer senselessly ends his life.

programme.title: Kiss Me Once
programme.desc: Gabrielle, a delusional romantic, fixates on anyone who shows her the slightest interest. Her obsession turns dangerous when her feelings aren't reciprocated.

programme.title: Meli
programme.desc: After finding an eviction notice on her door, Meli decides it’s time to up the ante and take matters into her own hands to protect her family by any means necessary. Out of pure desperation she attempts to burglarize a stranger’s luxurious home.

programme.title: Don't Miss the Bus
programme.desc: Sierra, a single mother hustles in order to survive with long shifts and grunt work. Her faith is pinned on lottery tickets and men who exploit her vulnerability. When a stroke of luck has the ability to change her future forever, a missed bus stop could mean life or death.

programme.title: ToxiCity
programme.desc: Corey Spencer, the innovator behind the successful ATLUV dating app, navigates a complex personal life. Despite his app's mission, he struggles with love, further complicated by a child support order. A night out leads him to Sierra, an alluring actress who quickly moves in. Their romance sours as Sierra's past as a dancer and her aggressive jealousy come to light.

programme.title: Transcend
programme.desc: Maranda spent her life looking for a protector, only to find herself lost in a cycle of toxic love and betrayal. From the wreckage of abandonment to a life-altering crossroads, her survival isn't just about escaping the past, it’s about surviving herself.

programme.title: Road Wars: Max Fury
programme.desc: In a post-apocalyptic wasteland, sisters try to save their mother armed only with a muscle car and ammo.

programme.title: King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table
programme.desc: In this modern retelling of the classic tale, Sorceress Morgana returns after 1,500 years, vowing destruction.

programme.title: Ape x Mecha Ape: New World Order
programme.desc: Mankind's last hope rests on Abraham and Mecha Ape MkII to defeat a new enemy.

programme.title: Shark Warning!
programme.desc: A man returns home years after his brother was killed by a shark, but the shark is back. He must get revenge.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: Our Heroes make their way to the greenhouse where "Batch 47" is being grown.

programme.title: Disaster Strike Force
programme.desc: A devastating deep freeze threatens the East Coast.

programme.title: Devil Dog Hound of Hell
programme.desc: Mans best friend turns out to be a family nightmare. The story centers around a suburban family and the harrowing experiences they endure from a possessed dog they innocently adopt. A household pet is conveniently killed, only to be replaced by a new dog, who turns out to be evil incarnate. Betty Barry and her two children fall under the dogs spell, until father and husband Mike gets some useful advice from an Ecuadorian shaman, which helps him send the beast back to hell.

programme.title: Dead Dudes In The House
programme.desc: Friends go inspect an old house. They find a grave of a woman in the front yard. One of them smashes the headstone and awakens her vengeance. The dead come alive and the living can't escape as the house locks down to keep them in, forever.

programme.title: Nightbreed: The Director's Cut (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Boone may be a troubled young man, but his troubles are just beginning. Set up as the fall guy in a string of slasher murders, he decides he'll hide by crossing the threshold that separates us from them and sneak into the forbidden subterranean realm of Midian. Boone will live among the monsters. Includes over 40 minutes of new footage, all mastered in high definition from the original camera negative!

programme.title: The Island
programme.desc: Herm and Evelin scheme against and betray their parents in the name of love, but this leads to further disaster as the virus claims another victim.

programme.title: The Island
programme.desc: As Mikkel loses his handle on the island and people ignore the restrictions, events begin to spiral out of control.

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: Creepy houses, murderous rednecks, final girls: horror films often rely on these tried-and-true storytelling devices, and audiences have come to expect them. What are the most common genre tropes and clichés? How do modern filmmakers subvert them?

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: Nearly a century after King Kong first ran amok, images of giant marauding monsters continue to enthrall us. Godzilla is hotter than ever, and filmmakers like Bong Joon-ho, J.J. Abrams, and Jordan Peele have all added their unique spins to the genre.

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: Veering between slow dread and shocking violence, the wild world of Japanese horror has something for everyone. From Ugetsu to Ring, Onibaba to Audition, Tetsuo to Battle Royale: the greatest J-Horror films are essential viewing for horror fans.

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: Horror comedies make us laugh at things we might recoil from in real life. They come in many flavors, including splatstick (Evil Dead II), high camp (M3GAN), subversive "family entertainment" (Gremlins) and stone-cold classics (Shaun of the Dead).

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: Stephen King's stories attract the world's greatest filmmakers, and have been the basis of over a dozen horror classics - and at least as many failures. What elements need to come together to successfully adapt King on the page to King on the screen?

programme.title: Quicksand
programme.desc: A married couple almost divorcing become trapped in quicksand while hiking through a rainforest in Colombia. They battle the elements of the jungle and must work together to survive.

programme.title: What Josiah Saw
programme.desc: A family with buried secrets reunite at a farmhouse after two decades to pay for their past sins.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale wants to get rid of the Clampetts before the arrival of Mrs. Smith-Standish, the head of a 'first family' historical society. But Mrs. Smith-Standish goes to the Clampetts' first and becomes enchanted with their antiques and way of life, even to roping the horrified Mrs. Drysdale into helping do the chores the old-fashioned way.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Becomes a Banker" - Drysdale hires Jed to be a banker when he needs a crack shot to help beat a rival banker in a skeet shooting competition, but his partner needs to be an employee.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Buys the Freeway" - Henry Jones shows up at the Clampett's, pretends to be an old friend and tries to sell the family the Hollywood Bowl, Griffith Park, and the freeway.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Steals a Wife" - Jed and Duke his dog are both down without a woman in their lives, until they see and pursue a French woman and her poodle that she brought to breed with Mrs. Drysdales dog. Meanwhile Mrs. Drysdale has arranged a "marriage" between the dogs, complete with a decorated bedroom.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Plays Solomon" - Granny can't stand Pearl's yodeling so she reports her to the police. When one of the officers takes Pearl out, Granny thinks she got Pearl in trouble and becomes sad and contrite. Meanwhile, the police find her still, and new trouble results.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Throws a Wingding" - Flatt and Scruggs and their wives come to visit their old sweetheart, but Pearl thinks they're there to propose. Jed throws a wingding for them and the duo play music for the Clampetts.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly's Animals" - When Pearl starts selling music lessons, Mrs. Drysdale complains about the noise to the police. Mrs. Drysdale also calls the dog catcher on Duke.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

Elmer Clifton (Assassin of Youth, Seven Doors to Death) directs a memorable cast that includes Rex Lease (The Sign of the Wolf, The Monster Walks), Lona Andre (Slaves in Bondage, Death in the Air), and William Farnum (Samson and Delilah). Meanwhile, Frank McGlynn Jr is in the title role of General George A. Custer.

programme.title: West of the Divide
programme.desc: John Wayne stars as Ted Hayden, a tough hombre who goes undercover to join a gang led by a heartless fiend known only as Mr. Gentry (Lloyd Whitlock). Gentry is out to take over a ranch belonging to one of Hayden's friends, but as Hayden finds out, Gentry has a lot more than that up his sleeve. You'll have to watch this two-fisted Western to find out the rest!

programme.title: Randy Rides Alone
programme.desc: John Wayne was 27 when he starred in this vintage western drama from Hollywoods Poverty Row. He plays Randy Bowers, a man wrongly jailed for murders. With help from a female friend, he breaks out of prison and sets out to find the real killers. Harry L. Fraser (The Last of the Clintons, Rustlers Paradise) directs a strong cast of familiar faces, including legendary sidekick George Gabby Hayes, Earl Dwyer, and one of the hardest ridin stuntmen turned actors, Yakima Canutt.

programme.title: Daughter of the West
programme.desc: A FLAMING PAGE FROM AMERICAN HISTORY Becomes a powerful motion picture...set against the panoramic grandeur of the West!. Martha Vickers stars as Lolita Moreno, a convent raised woman in the 1880s who learns about her Native American heritage through her relationship with an educated Navajo. Morena had been raised at a California mission, but when she leaves to teach school in Arizona, the threads of her mysterious past are revealed. This leads to her involvement with some dangerous people, and a chance to find love in the most unexpected way. The cast includes Phillip Reed, Donald Woods, William Farnum and Milton Kibbee. Pedro de Cordobaa stars as Chief Wykomas.

programme.title: American Empire
programme.desc: This epic of America's westward march stars Leo Carrillo and Preston Foster in a tale of men building a cattle empire, only to battle rustlers, the weather, and each other! The romantic adventure from director William C. McGann (In Old California, Alcatraz Island) features a strong leading performance by Oscar nominated RKO star Richard Dix (Cimarron, The Ghost Ship) plus cinematography by six-time Oscar nominee Russell Harlan (To Kill a Mockingbird, Rio Bravo).

programme.title: The Legend of the Lone Ranger

With 12 athletes at the start over 6 cruel events, like Marble Stone Lift, Truckpull and Deadlift cars. And with the comeback of Travis Ortmayer / USA, former stone king and world ranked competitor. Winner of several SCL stages in the past.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Four times Strongest Man of Holland, Alex Moonen and the “Flying Dutchman” Kelvin de Ruiter competing here in Holland against 12 other strongest men in the world. There were 6 events in total, and 3 in this episode: The first event, a truckpull of 17.000 kg over 25 meters in max 75 seconds, was a real hard pull Also for the World Champion Truckpull, Holland’s runner-up Kelvin de Ruiter, but watch his amazing technique here.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Strongest Man of Holland, Alex Moonen and the “Flying Dutchman” Kelvin de Ruiter, competing in Holland against 12 other strongest men in the world. There were 6 events in total, 3 in this second episode; a Viking-press of 150 kg with the current world record holder Luke Herrick. The fifth event, a front hold of 25 kg is loved or hated by the athletes and lastly, the Stones of Strength.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: An unbelievable TANK PULL of 50.000 kg was at the stage of SCL RUSSIA. Never seen this before! Not only a tank pull was done here but also the Viking Press/ Deadlift Max and Log lift reps were spectacular events to see here in Russia. With Mikhail Koklyaev also as event presenter, the former top 3 world strongest man from Russia was there

programme.title: Strongman Champions League

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Mateusz Kieliszkowski from Poland and Aivars Smaukstelis from Latvia are the strongman future of these days. Mateusz is the best man in the World at the moment to defeat. Only 25 years and such a fantastic strongman. They come here only for a win. But there are 12 more Strong athletes in this episode who want the title of Finland as well.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Mateusz Is leading after day 1 (previous episode) with close behind him Aivars Smaukstelis and Zake Muluzi from the UK. All very exciting! 5 events in this episode: Log Lift, Fram Walk, Deadlift, Sack Throw (Weight for Height), Loading medley.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: In this stage we have a new strong debutant: Oleg Sylka from the Ukraine. He is the new talent, who will stand up against the old champions like ZSydrunas Savickas and Brian Shaw and the Stoltman Brother from the UK. This new talent Oleg is a great character and we will see him flame in the episode in Portugal.

New routines such as Coming Home at 4am have already become cult faves and Bruer favorites like The Party have received a musical facelift. Jim's uncanny impressions, such as Brian Johnson of AC/DC, are also included.

programme.title: Living Legends Of Comedy: Real Talk

See and hear the Kings and Queens of comedy like never before in a jaw-dropping inside glimpse of the raw cut-throat comedy game. This documentary contains exclusive interviews with living legends D.L. Hughley, Earthquake, Sheryl Underwood, Don DC Curry, Charlie Murphy, Kevin Hart, Rich Vos, Donnell Rawlings, Showtime at the Apollo Founder Chuck Sutton and many more. Living Legends also contains hilarious stand-up comedy performances.

Defiant, socially relevant, and always hilarious, it's a legendary Mooney performance.

programme.title: A Fantastic Fear Of Everything

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Chewed Up crew sets up poolside at the W Hotel South Beach for a breezy St. Patrick's Day episode that swaps corned beef for Cuban grilled shrimp. Michael Symon, whose Cleveland neighborhood is deeply Irish, admits he's been celebrating St. Patrick's Day with his best friend Jimmy for decades, and this year Jimmy joins them in Miami. Michael's Grilled Shrimp Mojo is a tribute to the Cuban flavors of Miami: shrimp marinated in lime zest, lime juice, chili flakes, and olive oil, then grilled and finished with a vibrant mojo sauce blended from sour orange, onion, a whole head of garlic, cilantro, parsley, and oregano. The key tip: cook shrimp until they look like commas, not O'sóthe moment they curl completely is the moment they're overdone. Clinton reflects on his seven years living in Miami and the city's culinary depth, from Cuban staples to modern restaurants like Yardbird. Carla raises her fear of sharks; Clinton counters with his inexplicable terror of birds getting caught in his hair. Michael confesses he bought a bathing suit specifically to go to the beach, put it on, saw himself in the mirror, and decided to spend the afternoon in bed instead.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Just in time for National Ravioli Day, acclaimed chef and Chopped judge Scott Conant joins the trio at the W South Beach to make his delicate ricotta gnocchiótechnically gnudióalongside his signature Martone Street jarred tomato sauce. Michael has been close friends with Scott for 15 years, and Clinton admits he was genuinely nervous before meeting the notoriously stern Chopped judge, quickly discovering he's warm and funny in person. Scott explains that Martone Street is named for his grandparents' home in Waterbury, Connecticut, where Sunday dinners taught him that conversation over food is one of life's greatest gifts. The gnocchi dough combines fresh ricotta, basil, breadcrumbs, flour, egg, and Parmesan, shaped into small dumplings and cooked until they float. Scott builds a stabilized butter sauce using pasta water and cornstarch as an emulsifieróa trick that keeps it smooth for hours. Finished with Martone Street sauce and broccoli rabe, the result is stunning in its simplicity. His philosophy runs throughout: spaghetti aglio e olio, done correctly, is one of the most complex and satisfying dishes imaginable. Scott becomes emotional talking about watching his 16-year-old daughter drive for the first time and the joy of steak cook-offs with his girls at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Food Network Star winner and Southern food authority Damaris Phillips brings a taste of Louisville poolside at the W South Beach, demonstrating three Southern tea sandwich classics that are anything but fussy. Carla Hall has been singing Damaris's praises for yearsómutual friend Jackie Guido insisted they were destined to be peas and carrotsóand the episode proves her right, the two radiating warmth and joy throughout. Damaris makes Pimento Cheese; Benedictine, a Louisville specialty of grated cucumber with dill and cream cheese (the key being squeezing out all the cucumber water first); and Ham Salad, essentially chicken salad's porky cousin, versatile as either sweet or savory. Everything is layered on Japanese milk bread or thin white bread, crusts removed, and assembled into dainty finger sandwiches and triple-decker stacks. Michael immediately tries to build an enormous open-face sandwich rather than the delicate bites Damaris intended, sparking a lively debate about proportion and restraint. Carla shares that she recently spent a cozy Super Sunday with Damaris in Louisvilleóa beloved tradition of soups, board games, and community. The episode also features a spirited group effort to talk Damaris out of her plan to get a leopard-print face tattoo, with limited success. The episode ends with genuine laughter, warm hugs, and unanimous agreement: the sandwiches are delicious.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Two-time Tournament of Champions winner and Chopped veteran Maneet Chauhan arrives at the W South Beach as a true Queen of Flavor, demonstrating her Indian Spiced Ceviche, a dish that applies Indian culinary logic to coastal Latin technique. Fresh fish is cured in lime juice in the traditional ceviche manner, then transformed with coconut milk to soften the heat and balance the citrus, and finished with a tarka: the Indian technique of blooming whole spices in searing hot coconut oil and pouring it directly over the dish at the last second. The result is layered, bright, and unexpected. Maneet explains that she grew up in a small town in India and learned to cook by exploring her neighbors' kitchens, each representing a different regional tradition. She recounts a pivotal story from Peru, where she made potato pancakes alongside a local woman without sharing a single word of common languageóconnected entirely through food and gesture. That philosophy runs through her Nashville restaurant, which introduces diners to bold Indian flavors through fusion dishes like hot chicken korma and chicken tikka salad poutine. She draws a parallel between the tarka and the French beurre blancóboth finishing techniques that transform a dish in the final seconds of cooking. Michael, Clinton, and Carla taste the ceviche with genuine surprise and awe.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: As the Chewed Up crew wraps up their week at the W South Beach, Miami-born chef Mika Leon brings the most authentically local dish of the entire trip: Pan Con Lechon, the slow-roasted Cuban pork sandwich that anchors Noche Buena, Cuban Christmas Eve. Mika woke up at 4 AM to start the pork shoulderómarinated overnight in a mojo sauce built on sour orange juice, lime juice, orange juice, and 50 cloves of garlic from her grandfather's recipe. After six hours at 350 degrees, the pork is shredded and crisped with additional mojo in a hot pan, then piled high on a special Cuban rollósweeter and fattier than regular bread, made with lard throughout. Mika shares her journey from dabbling in TV production (she quit after a week) to opening a food truck selling $5 Pan Con Lechons at 3 AM to Miami club-goers, which grew into a full restaurant and now a cookbook. Carla notes that the best cooks often learn from family before any formal training, and this dish proves it. The group discusses Miami's cultural identityómore its own country than a Florida cityódefined by Spanglish, its own traffic logic, and immigrant food traditions passed down for generations. Clinton finally admits that despite a full week at the W, none of them ever made it into the pool.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The South Beach week closes with a live audience tapingóthe most unhinged episode of the tripóas Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly perform in front of a crowd of fans. Michael's voice is noticeably shot from a long week of celebrating, so Clinton and Carla carry extra energy while he freestyle hosts from notes he promptly loses within 30 seconds. Carla makes her refreshing Pineapple Lime Agua Fresca: blended fresh pineapple with lime water (lime juice diluted to avoid sharpness), ginger, honey, and wateróa batched mocktail or cocktail mixer that's as beautiful as it is easy. She shares her tip for getting full use of citrus by zesting before juicing. The episode features 'Chew It or Screw It,' a voting game where the audience weighs in on Florida's most adventurous foods: iguana pizza, python sausage, alligator ribs, conch fritters, and fried frog legs. Carla and Clinton report on their spontaneous 7 AM sunrise bike rideótheir first ever togetheróand Clinton jokes about his impossibly pale, non-tanning legs. The trio explores Florida's wildlife peculiarities, from iguanas falling from trees in cold snaps to flying cockroaches given affectionate nicknames. A Chewed Up Challenge heads-up guessing game rounds out the audience interaction with giveaways. Season 3's Miami week ends exactly how it began: loud, funny, and hungry.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Season 3 returns to the studio with a spring episode that puts handmade pasta front and center. Michael Symon makes his grandmother's Cavatellióa ricotta-based pasta dough (1 cup flour, 1 egg, 1.5 cups ricotta, Parmesan) that is more forgiving than traditional egg pasta, closer in spirit to a dumpling than a proper noodle. He demonstrates the technique his grandmother taught him as a child: roll the dough into thin strands, cut into inch-long pieces, and press-roll each one with a butter knife to create a hollow, ridged shape that opens slightly when cooked and catches every drop of sauce. Carla has a revelation mid-episode realizing cavatelli is essentially a dumplingóthe Italian version of a global tradition that spans every culture. The sauce is spring itself: brown butter carried almost to the edge, blanched peas, bias-cut asparagus, pasta water, and lemon. Michael also reflects on the memory of cooking as familyópasta making as a group project, a Sunday activity with kids or grandkids, something imperfect that matters anyway. The episode weaves in conversations about spring weather chaos on the East Coast, Gen X collectively aging past the Golden Girls cast, Michael's habit of texting Cleveland friends his Venice Beach weather reports, and a team-meeting story where Nick's extended hand produced entirely different reactions from all three hosts based on their respective cultural backgrounds. Nick debuts a new segment, 'Choose Wisely,' a spring-edition 'most likely to' game. Sponsored by Fresh Direct and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Top Chef alum and Carla Hall's culinary producer Ariane Duarte joins the Chewed Up crew to make spring crostini three ways on homemade sourdough focaccia, while the hosts debate food rules and play Chew It or Screw It.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall makes a vibrant frozen pea soup in under 20 minutes—sautéed onions, stock, mint, parsley, white pepper, and cream blended silky smooth—then leads the hosts in Peep Roulette, a blind flavor-guessing game. Clinton wins; Michael is traumatized.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this “Munch Madness” episode of Chewed Up, Carla Hall, Clinton Kelly, and Michael Symon put eight frozen pizzas through a bracket-style taste test inspired by March Madness. The hosts compare each pairing, breaking down crust, sauce, cheese, and overall flavor while mixing in humor, nostalgia, and brutally honest reactions. As they move through the rounds, the conversation is fueled by strong opinions, unexpected preferences, and plenty of debate about what actually makes a good pizza. Michael’s love of authentic pizza drives passionate critiques, Clinton reflects on his long hiatus from frozen options, and Carla keeps things grounded in everyday practicality for busy households. The competition builds toward a final showdown that sparks lively discussion and a few surprising takes. The episode then shifts into a conversation about potlucks, outdoor dining, and picnics, blending personal preferences with playful arguments. It wraps with a fan giveaway and the hosts’ signature banter.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly welcomes Stacy London for a fun, nostalgic episode focused on creative ways to use leftover hard-boiled eggs. Stacy makes her signature deviled eggs with a creamy filling and bold toppings like chorizo and jalapeño, while Clinton shares a simple asparagus mimosa finished with grated eggs and vinaigrette. Along the way, the hosts swap stories, talk fashion, and play games, bringing humor and energy to an episode that blends easy recipes with playful conversation.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Hometown Week brings Michael Symon's roots to the Chewed Up kitchen, as he makes his grandfather Pap's potato and farmer's cheese pierogis—a dish that traces back to the heavy Eastern European influence in both Cleveland, where Michael was born, and Pittsburgh, where his parents grew up. The dough is Pap's recipe: all-purpose flour, a pinch of salt, one egg, sour cream for tenderness, and softened butter worked in for extra fat and pliability—a forgiving dough that rests in the fridge for an hour before being rolled and cut into rounds. The filling is pure simplicity: Idaho potato riced and folded with farmer's cheese in a 2:1 ratio, seasoned with chives, salt, and pepper. Michael boils the pierogis until they float, then finishes them in a pan with caramelized onions, butter, a splash of pasta water, and fresh chives—garnished tableside with sour cream. The episode's centerpiece story is the birth of Lola's famous beef cheek pierogi: when it first went on the Cleveland menu in 1997, diners balked at 'beef cheek,' and Michael sold only one or two a night. He renamed it 'pot roast pierogi,' sold 70 the next night, ran it for a year until diners were hooked, then quietly switched the name back to beef cheek. It became the restaurant's most popular appetizer by a factor of ten. Carla mentions that her nephew AJ is addicted to Michael's GoldBelly pierogis—available in potato and cheese, pork and sauerkraut, and beef cheek. The episode features three debate topics: leftovers vs. the original meal (unanimous chew it, with the exception of reheated steak and fish); the simplest dishes being the hardest to execute (cacio e pepe, French omelet, pie crust, biscuits, vanilla ice cream); and breakfast for dinner (universal love, best kept occasional—or as a late-night eggs tradition after a couple of old fashioneds). The game is Michael's Two Truths and a Lie: he slipped on butter and dropped a full tray at Lola (true); he set off a fire alarm while cooking at a friend's house (true); and he once cooked pierogis on a George Foreman Grill and melted the plastic drip tray (the lie—Clinton guesses correctly, consistent with Michael's famous contempt for George Foremans, air fryers, and microwaves). Fan mail covers: favorite cake (Clinton: carrot cake with raisins; Michael: coconut; Carla: German chocolate); what to do with canned crab (Michael: fold it into a pierogi filling or make angry crab pasta; Carla: red sauce pasta with crab); and why chefs hate tilapia (muddy flavor, overcrowded farming, general bad juju). The fan giveaway offers a monogrammed Michael Symon apron from his Frontgate collection and a copy of Simply Symon Suppers—won by Sue from Columbus, Georgia. Sponsored by FreshDirect and Front Gate.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - Dave's Comfort Foods: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. This episode sees Dave working on a "Classic Comfort Foods" menu for NBA legends Reggie Miller and Mark Jackson. Dishes include: Caesar salad, New England clam chowder, fettuccine alfredo, salmon with caper-butter sauce, a potato rösti, and a chocolate tart.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: Dave and Chris Ying sit down to work out a menu for this week's guest in the studio: artisanal baker, New Yorker by way of New Orleans, allergic-to-shellfish friend of the show, Bryan Ford.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: With a New Orleans–inspired menu now set for his friend Bryan Ford, Dave starts testing out this week’s recipes: barbecue turkey legs, meat pies, coconut flatbread, and coconut beignets.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show

programme.title: Wide World of Cooking
programme.desc: Bernie’s has turned a simple frozen dessert into a new LA classic: the granita with toasted meringue. Chef Jon Berne layers bright, icy fruit with a cloud of brûléed meringue on top — part snow cone, part pie topping, part pure summertime magic. In this episode, he takes us behind the counter to show how this deceptively simple treat became a Smorgasburg obsession, one spoonful at a time.

programme.title: Wide World of Cooking
programme.desc: There are breakfast burritos, and then there’s Macheen’s breakfast burrito — a tortilla wrapped around the imagination of chef Jonathan Pérez and his sister, Ana. Birria and swiss, longanisa, and crispy pork belly breakfast burritos are the true stars at Macheen. In this episode, they show us how their spin on the classics has turned into one of Smorgasburg’s most talked-about stalls.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Chef Walter Staib takes a journey Down Under to Sydney, Australia, where he learns of the regenerative techniques Australian Farmers use to create delicious-tasting Beef and support a sustainable future for the planet. Get ready for a sizzling adventure that celebrates the flavors of Australia in true "Barbie" style!

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Join us on a spirited journey as we explore the rich history of Applejack and Apple Brandy, discovering their colonial origins and witnessing the meticulous distillation process at America's oldest distillery. Chef Walter Staib returns to the historic Harriton House to fire up some apple-inspired recipes, including Scallopini of Pork with Stuffing, a sizzling Apple Flambé, and more.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Today is all about sustainability; eating in a way that’s good for us and the planet. Diane cooks up a colorful, mostly plant-based feast that celebrates fresh, local ingredients, big flavor, and a small footprint! She’ll discuss the Mediterranean way of eating with legendary Greek physician, scientist, and nutritionist, Dr. Antonia Trichopoulou.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Grains are the feel-good food of the Greek and Mediterranean diet, providing nutrition, versatility and soul-warming comfort. Diane visits Pnyka bakery where they grind their own flour from an ancient strain of grain. In the kitchen, Diane shares her knowledge and love of grains with 3 easy Greek-Mediterranean recipes.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Beans are a superfood linked to longevity and play a starring role in Greek cuisine. Diane explores dried vs. canned beans, visits an Athens legume shop, and chats with Dr. William Li on their health benefits. She shares satisfying, easy-to-make recipes to help you eat well and feel great.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: In the traditional Greek and Mediterranean diet, seasonal dishes feature naturally probiotic ingredients such as pickled vegetables, olives and yogurt. Diane explores these probiotic foods and shares ways to incorporate them into everyday recipes. Over tea, she discusses the benefits of probiotic foods with Dr. Theoklis Zaoutis.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Phyllo has been part of Greek cuisine for centuries and provides an easy backdrop to countless dishes, from appetizers to desserts. Diane has fun with phyllo creating; a crunchy topping to a spanakopita salad, a crispy phyllo pizza and finally a true Greek classic – baklava.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati is in the magic town of Maní to learn about something sacred to the Mayans - bees and honey. A cooperative of women who protect the endangered Melipona, a stingless bee native to Yucatán, invites her to join in a Mayan energy cleansing ritual involving buñuelos made with Melipona honey. Then she visits a family living by the traditional "Solar Maya," meaning they grow everything they eat.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: In Tekit, or "The Capital of the Guayabera," Pati encounters a tale of sons going against their father's wishes to follow their own path. Pati meets Eliodoro Xicum, a farmer's son who went against his father to build a family business making the iconic Guayabera shirts. Then she meets his son, Elio, who chose to become a chef and created a dish that resembles a Guayabera in honor of his father.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati explores the vibrant streets of Yucatán's capital, Mérida, in search of its flavors. She tries "the best tacos in Mérida" at Wayan'e. The aroma of freshly made cookies lures her to Dondé Fabric, where their globitos and bizcochitos are an important part of Mérida's mornings. She has sorbet at a shop run by the same family for generations and tastes a unique Yucatecan liqueur.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits Chef Wilson Alonzo in his hometown of Halachó to prepare a traditional Cochinita Pibil in an underground pit. They begin at the local market to source ingredients. Then Pati helps Wilson prepare the achiote marinade, along with his grandmother who taught him traditional cooking and isn't shy to correct him. The final product is a meal that captures Yucatán's history in every bite.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati arrives in Motul, a town in the history books for Yucatán's once thriving henequén industry and for the egg dish Huevos Motuleños. Pati meets Doña Evelia, who put Motul back on the map with her world-famous recipe. She also experiences the legacy of henequén at Hacienda Tamchén and encounters another egg dish in the kitchen, where chef Julio Ku Dominguez makes Abuela's Buttoned Eggs.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Temozón is the birthplace of Yucatán's signature smoked meat Carne Ahumada and everyone in town claims to have a relative who invented it. Pati strolls around town to try different versions of Carne Ahumada. She also returns to Hacienda Tamchén for another traditional dish prepared by chef Julio Ku Dominguez called Huidzi Bii Wai, which means "united tortilla."

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati returns to Mérida to meet sisters, Delia and Maria Elide, who love to cook and laugh and are famous for recados - pastes of spices and aromatic herbs that season Yucatecan foods. In Uxmal, she learns about ingredients only found in Yucatán that make recados unique, touring citrus, habanero, and chaya fields at an hacienda. Then traditional cook Rosa makes a Relleno Negro using recado negro.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Today no trip to Yucatán is complete without seeing a beautiful cenote, natural freshwater pools in caves. But Mayans saw them as the gateway to the underworld. To learn more about Mayan communities, Pati visits Cenote Xocempich with activist and lawyer Zoila Cen, who has dedicated her career to helping Mayan people. The next day, Zoila invites Pati to her niece's birthday celebration.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: An ingredient Pati constantly uses is salt and Yucatán has one of the most unique salts. The Las Coloradas salt, which means blush red, gets its distinctive color from red algae. Engineer Felipe Perez takes Pati to the pink lakes where the salt is produced. Down the road in Rio Lagartos, conservationist Diego Nuñez takes her to see another pink wonder, Mexico's highest concentration of flamingos.

programme.desc: A night of karaoke results in a domestic-violence charge, and a drunk inmate starts a fight with his cellmate.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Officers find hidden drugs on a burglary suspect during a pat down that leads to a tense confrontation. programme.desc: After refusing a breathalyzer test, an irate man bangs his head against plexiglass. A man transforms into a “cell warrior” within his holding pen. Soon to be released, an inmate considers the future.
